debug_info.goal:
H : Type u_1
inst✝² : NormedAddCommGroup H
inst✝¹ : InnerProductSpace ℂ H
inst✝ : CompleteSpace H
a : H →L[ℂ] H
ha : IsSelfAdjoint a
x y : H
f : C(↑(spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)
⊢ ‖ComplexMeasure.integral (@cfc_toComplexMeasure H inst✝² inst✝¹ inst✝ a ha x y) ⇑f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖

failed_attempts:
have h1 : ComplexMeasure.integral (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y) f = cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f := by
    simp [cfc_toComplexMeasure, ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure_apply, cfc_toLinearFunctional]
  rw [h1]
  have h2 : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by
    have h3 : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f‖ = ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ * ‖f‖ := by
      rw [norm_mul]
    rw [h3]
    have h4 : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound x y
    have h5 : ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ = (‖x‖ * ‖y‖) * ‖f‖ := by ring
    rw [h5]
    apply mul_le_mul_of_nonneg_right h4
    positivity
  linarith

have h1 : ComplexMeasure.integral (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y) ⇑f = ⟪x, (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f) y⟫_ℂ := by
    simp [cfc_toComplexMeasure, ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure_apply]
  rw [h1]
  have h2 : ‖⟪x, (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f) y⟫_ℂ‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖(cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f) y‖ := by
    apply norm_inner_le_norm
  have h3 : ‖(cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f) y‖ ≤ ‖cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f‖ * ‖y‖ := by
    apply le_of_eq
    rw [norm_apply_le_norm_mul_norm]
  have h4 : ‖cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f‖ = ‖f‖ := by
    simp [norm_cfcHom]
  calc
    ‖⟪x, (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f) y⟫_ℂ‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖(cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f) y‖ := h2
    _ ≤ ‖x‖ * (‖cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f‖ * ‖y‖) := by
      apply mul_le_mul_of_nonneg_left h3 (by positivity)
    _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f‖ := by ring
    _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by rw [h4]

have h1 : (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f =
      cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f := by
    simp [cfc_toComplexMeasure, ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure, cfc_toLinearFunctional]
    <;> try { tauto }
    <;> try { norm_num }
    <;> try { aesop }
    <;> try { tauto }
  rw [h1]
  have h2 : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by
    have h3 : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
      apply ContinuousLinearMap.opNorm_le_bound
      · positivity
      · intro f'
        simp only [cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply]
        calc
          ‖⟪x, ((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f') y⟫_ℂ‖
            ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f') y‖ := by
              apply norm_inner_le_norm
              all_goals try { tauto }
              all_goals try { aesop }
            _ ≤ ‖x‖ * (‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f')‖ * ‖y‖) := by
              apply mul_le_mul_of_nonneg_left
              · -- Show ‖(cfcHom ... f') y‖ ≤ ‖cfcHom ... f'‖ * ‖y‖
                have h5 : ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f') y‖ ≤ ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f')‖ * ‖y‖ := by
                  apply norm_le_norm_of_le
                  all_goals try { tauto }
                  all_goals try { aesop }
                  all_goals try { tauto }
                nlinarith [h5]
              · norm_nonneg x
            _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖((cfcHom (IsSelfAdjoint.isStarNormal ha)) f')‖ := by ring
            _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f'‖ := by rw [norm_cfcHom a f']
    have h4 : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f‖ = ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ * ‖f‖ := by
      rw [norm_eq_norm_mul_norm]
      all_goals simp [cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply]
      all_goals try { tauto }
      all_goals try { aesop }
      all_goals try { tauto }
    rw [h4]
    nlinarith [h3, norm_nonneg f, norm_nonneg (cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y)]
  exact h2

    have h3 : ‖innerSL ℂ x ((cfcCLM ha f) y)‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖(cfcCLM ha f) y‖ := by
      apply norm_inner_le_norm
    have h4 : ‖(cfcCLM ha f) y‖ ≤ ‖f‖ * ‖y‖ := by
      calc
        ‖(cfcCLM ha f) y‖ = ‖cfcCLM ha f y‖ := by simp
        _ ≤ ‖cfcCLM ha f‖ * ‖y‖ := by
          apply norm_le_norm_apply
        _ = ‖f‖ * ‖y‖ := by
          simp [norm_cfcHom a f]
    have h5 : ‖x‖ * ‖(cfcCLM ha f) y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * (‖f‖ * ‖y‖) := by
      apply mul_le_mul_of_nonneg_left h4
      all_goals positivity
    have h6 : ‖x‖ * (‖f‖ * ‖y‖) = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by ring
    linarith [h3, h5, h6]
  exact h2

have h2 : (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f = (cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y) f := by
    simp [cfc_toComplexMeasure, ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure_apply]
  rw [h2]
  have h3 : ‖(cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y) f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by
    have h4 : ‖(cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y) f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by
      calc
        ‖(cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y) f‖ = ‖⟪x, (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f) y⟫_ℂ‖ := by
          simp [cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply]
        _ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖(cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f) y‖ := by
          apply norm_inner_le_norm
        _ ≤ ‖x‖ * (‖cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f‖ * ‖y‖) := by
          apply mul_le_mul_of_nonneg_left
          exact le_opNorm (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f) y
          all_goals norm_nonneg x
        _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f‖ := by ring
        _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by
          rw [norm_cfcHom a f]
    exact h4
  exact h3

have h1 : ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ = ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f‖ := by

  rw [h1]
  have h2 : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f‖ ≤ ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ * ‖f‖ := by
    apply ContinuousLinearMap.norm_le
  have h3 : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by

  have h4 : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f‖ ≤ (‖x‖ * ‖y‖) * ‖f‖ := by
    nlinarith
  have h5 : (‖x‖ * ‖y‖) * ‖f‖ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by
    ring
  linarith [h4, h5]

have h1 : (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f = (cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y) f := by
    simp [cfc_toComplexMeasure, ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure_apply]
  rw [h1]
  have h2 : ‖(cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y) f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by
    have h3 : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
      apply cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound
    have h4 : ‖(cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y) f‖ ≤ ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ * ‖f‖ := by
      apply ContinuousLinearMap.norm_apply_le
    have h5 : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ * ‖f‖ ≤ (‖x‖ * ‖y‖) * ‖f‖ := by
      have h6 : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := h3
      nlinarith [h6, norm_nonneg f]
    calc
      ‖(cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y) f‖ ≤ ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ * ‖f‖ := h4
      _ ≤ (‖x‖ * ‖y‖) * ‖f‖ := h5
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by ring
  simpa using h2

have h1 : (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f = cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f := by
    simp [cfc_toComplexMeasure, ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure_integrate]
  rw [h1]
  have h2 : cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f = ⟪x, (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f) y⟫_ℂ := by
    simp [cfc_toLinearFunctional]
  rw [h2]
  have h3 : ‖⟪x, (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f) y⟫_ℂ‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖(cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f) y‖ := by
    apply norm_inner_le_norm
  have h4 : ‖(cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f) y‖ ≤ ‖(cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f)‖ * ‖y‖ := by
    apply norm_le_norm_mul_norm
  have h5 : ‖(cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f)‖ = ‖f‖ := by
    simp [norm_cfcHom]
  calc
    ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ = ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f‖ := by
      rw [h1]
    _ = ‖⟪x, (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f) y⟫_ℂ‖ := by
      rw [h2]
    _ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖(cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f) y‖ := by
      apply norm_inner_le_norm
    _ ≤ ‖x‖ * (‖(cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f)‖ * ‖y‖) := by
      apply mul_le_mul_of_nonneg_left
      exact h4
      all_goals
        norm_nonneg x
    _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by
      rw [h5]
      all_goals
        ring

have h1 : (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f = cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f := by
    simp [cfc_toComplexMeasure, ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure, cfc_toLinearFunctional]
  rw [h1]
  have h2 : cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f = ⟪x, (cfcCLM ha f) y⟫_ℂ := by
    simp [cfc_toLinearFunctional, cfcCLM_apply]
  rw [h2]
  have h3 : ‖⟪x, (cfcCLM ha f) y⟫_ℂ‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖(cfcCLM ha f) y‖ := by
    apply norm_inner_le_norm
  have h4 : ‖(cfcCLM ha f) y‖ ≤ ‖cfcCLM ha f‖ * ‖y‖ := by
    apply ContinuousLinearMap.le_opNorm
  have h5 : ‖x‖ * ‖(cfcCLM ha f) y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * (‖cfcCLM ha f‖ * ‖y‖) := by
    apply mul_le_mul_of_nonneg_left h4 (norm_nonneg x)
  have h6 : ‖x‖ * (‖cfcCLM ha f‖ * ‖y‖) = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖cfcCLM ha f‖ := by
    ring
  have h7 : ‖⟪x, (cfcCLM ha f) y⟫_ℂ‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖cfcCLM ha f‖ := by
    linarith [h3, h5, h6]
  have h8 : ‖cfcCLM ha f‖ = ‖f‖ := by
    simp [norm_cfcHom]
  have h9 : ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖cfcCLM ha f‖ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by
    rw [h8]
  linarith [h7, h9]

have h1 : (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f = ⟪x, (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f y)⟫_ℂ := by
    simp [cfc_toComplexMeasure, ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure_apply]
    <;> try { tauto }
  rw [h1]
  have h2 : ‖⟪x, (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f y)⟫_ℂ‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖(cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f y)‖ := by
    apply norm_inner_le_norm
  have h3 : ‖(cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f y)‖ ≤ ‖f‖ * ‖y‖ := by
    have h4 : ‖(cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f) y‖ ≤ ‖(cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f)‖ * ‖y‖ := by
      apply norm_le_mul_norm_of_norm_le
      all_goals
        try { norm_nonneg }
    have h5 : ‖(cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f)‖ = ‖f‖ := by
      simp [norm_cfcHom]
    rw [h5] at h4
    linarith
  have h4 : ‖x‖ * ‖(cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f y)‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * (‖f‖ * ‖y‖) := by
    have h5 : ‖(cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f y)‖ ≤ ‖f‖ * ‖y‖ := h3
    have h6 : ‖x‖ ≥ 0 := norm_nonneg x
    nlinarith [h5, h6]
  have h7 : ‖x‖ * (‖f‖ * ‖y‖) = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by
    ring
  linarith [h2, h4, h7]

have h1 : (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f = ⟪x, (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f) y⟫_ℂ := by
    simp [cfc_toComplexMeasure, ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure_apply]
    <;> simp [cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply]
  rw [h1]
  have h2 : ‖⟪x, (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f) y⟫_ℂ‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by
    have h3 : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by
      have h4 : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f‖ ≤ ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ * ‖f‖ := by
        apply norm_mul_le
      have h5 : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
        apply cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound
      have h6 : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f‖ ≤ (‖x‖ * ‖y‖) * ‖f‖ := by
        nlinarith
      ring_nf at h6 ⊢
      all_goals linarith
    have h7 : ‖⟪x, (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f) y⟫_ℂ‖ = ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f‖ := by
      simp [cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply]
    rw [h7]
    all_goals linarith
  exact h2

have h1 : ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖f‖ * ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ := by
    apply ComplexMeasure.norm_int_le_norm_f
  have h2 : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound x y
  have h3 : ‖f‖ * ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ ≤ ‖f‖ * (‖x‖ * ‖y‖) := by
    apply mul_le_mul_of_nonneg_left h2
    positivity
  have h4 : ‖f‖ * (‖x‖ * ‖y‖) = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by ring
  linarith [h1, h3, h4]

have h1 : (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f = cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f := by
    simp [cfc_toComplexMeasure, ComplexRMK.integral_apply]
    <;> try { 
      norm_num [cfc_toComplexMeasure, ComplexRMK.integral_apply]
    }
  rw [h1]
  have h3 : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by
    have h4 : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f‖ ≤ ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ * ‖f‖ := by
      apply norm_mul_le
    have h5 : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
      apply cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound
    have h6 : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ * ‖f‖ ≤ (‖x‖ * ‖y‖) * ‖f‖ := by
      have h7 : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := h5
      nlinarith [show 0 ≤ ‖f‖ by apply norm_nonneg]
    have h8 : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f‖ ≤ (‖x‖ * ‖y‖) * ‖f‖ := by
      linarith [h4, h6]
    calc
      ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f‖ ≤ (‖x‖ * ‖y‖) * ‖f‖ := h8
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by ring
  exact h3

have h1 : ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ = ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f‖ := by
    rw [ComplexRMK.integral_eq_of_zero_at_infty]
    · simp only [cfc_toComplexMeasure, cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply]
      all_goals simp
      all_goals try { tauto }
    all_goals simp
    all_goals try { tauto }
  rw [h1]
  have h2 : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by
    have h3 : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound x y
    have h4 : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f‖ ≤ ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ * ‖f‖ := by
      apply ContinuousLinearMap.norm_le
    have h5 : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ * ‖f‖ ≤ (‖x‖ * ‖y‖) * ‖f‖ := by
      have h6 : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := h3
      nlinarith [h6, norm_nonneg f]
    calc
      ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f‖ ≤ ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ * ‖f‖ := h4
      _ ≤ (‖x‖ * ‖y‖) * ‖f‖ := h5
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by ring
  exact h2
have h1 : (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f = cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f

simp [cfc_toComplexMeasure, ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure, cfcCLM]
  <;> try { 
    simp_all [toLinearFunctional_apply, cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply]
    <;> try { 
      rw [← ComplexRMK.integral_eq_inner]
      all_goals try { 
        simp_all [toLinearFunctional_apply, cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply]
        <;> try { 
          simp_all [cfcCLM_apply, cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply]
        }
      }
    }
  }
  <;> try { 
    simp_all [toLinearFunctional_apply, cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply]
    <;> try { 
      rw [← ComplexRMK.integral_eq_inner]
      all_goals try { 
        simp_all [toLinearFunctional_apply, cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply]
        <;> try { 
          simp_all [cfcCLM_apply, cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply]
        }
      }
    }
  }
```

Wait, that's too complicated. Let's simplify.

The definition of `cfc_toComplexMeasure` is `ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure (compSL C₀((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) ℂ _ _ (cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y) _)`.

The `compSL` is a continuous linear map from `C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)` to `ℂ`. The `cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y` is a continuous linear map from `C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)` to `ℂ`. So `compSL` is applying `cfc_toLinearFunctional` to the input function.

Wait, no. `compSL` is a composition of linear maps. Let's look at the definition:

`compSL C₀((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) ℂ _ _ (cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y) (@ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap.ContinuousMap.liftZeroAtInftyCLE (spectrum ℂ a) ℂ ℂ _ _ _ _ _ _).symm.toContinuousLinearMap`

Wait, `cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y` is a linear map from `C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)` to `ℂ`. The `compSL` is taking `cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y` as the first map and `apply' H _ y` as the second map. Wait, no, looking at the definition:

`compSL (H →L[ℂ] H) _ _ _ _ (innerSL ℂ x) (apply' H _ y)` for `toLinearFunctional`.

Similarly, `compSL C(spectrum ℂ a, ℂ) _ _ _ _ (toLinearFunctional x y) (cfcCLM ha)` for `cfc_toLinearFunctional`.

So for `h1`, we need to show `(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f = innerSL ℂ x ((cfcCLM ha f) y)`.

The definition of `cfc_toComplexMeasure` is `ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure (compSL C₀((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) ℂ _ _ (cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y) _)`.

The `compSL` is `compSL C₀((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) ℂ _ _ (cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y) (@ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap.ContinuousMap.liftZeroAtInftyCLE (spectrum ℂ a) ℂ ℂ _ _ _ _ _ _).symm.toContinuousLinearMap`.

Wait, `cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y` is a map `C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →L[ℂ] ℂ`. So `compSL` is `compSL (C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) _ _ _ _ (cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y) (apply' ℂ _ _)`? No.

Wait, looking at the definition of `cfc_toComplexMeasure`:

`ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure (compSL C₀((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) ℂ _ _ (cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y) (@ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap.ContinuousMap.liftZeroAtInftyCLE (spectrum ℂ a) ℂ ℂ _ _ _ _ _ _).symm.toContinuousLinearMap)`

This is `ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure (c)` where `c` is a continuous linear map from `C₀((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)` to `ℂ`.

The input to `ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure` is a continuous linear map `g : C₀((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →L[ℂ] ℂ`. The output is a complex measure `μ` such that `∫_{spec a} g dμ = g(1)`, where `g(1)` is the value of the linear map at the constant function `1`.

Wait, more precisely, `ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure` takes a continuous linear map `g : C₀(X, ℂ) →L[ℂ] ℂ` and returns a complex measure `μ` on `X` such that for any `f ∈ C₀(X, ℂ)`, `∫_X f dμ = g(f)`.

In our case, `g = cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y`, which is `compSL C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) _ _ _ _ (toLinearFunctional x y) (cfcCLM ha)`.

Wait, the definition of `cfc_toLinearFunctional` is `compSL C(spectrum ℂ a, ℂ) _ _ _ _ (toLinearFunctional x y) (cfcCLM ha)`. So this is `compSL` applied to `x, y` and `f` (the input function). So `cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f` is `innerSL ℂ x ((cfcCLM ha f) y)`.

Therefore, the integral `∫_{spec a} f dμ` should be equal to `innerSL ℂ x ((cfcCLM ha f) y)`.

Let's try to prove this:

```tactics
have h1 : (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f = innerSL ℂ x ((cfcCLM ha f) y) := by
  have h2 : (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f = 
    ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure (compSL C₀((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) ℂ _ _ (cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y) (@ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap.ContinuousMap.liftZeroAtInftyCLE (spectrum ℂ a) ℂ ℂ _ _ _ _ _ _).symm.toContinuousLinearMap) f := by
    simp [cfc_toComplexMeasure]
  rw [h2]
```

Now we need to show that `ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure c f = innerSL ℂ x ((cfcCLM ha f) y)` where `c = compSL C₀((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) ℂ _ _ (cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y) _`.

The definition of `ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure` applied to a linear map `L` is such that `∫_{spec a} g dμ = L(g)` for any `g ∈ C₀((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)`.

In our case, `L = cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y`, which is `compSL C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) _ _ _ _ (toLinearFunctional x y) (cfcCLM ha)`. So `L(f) = innerSL ℂ x ((cfcCLM ha f) y)`.

Therefore, `ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure L f = L(f) = innerSL ℂ x ((cfcCLM ha f) y)`.

So we need to show this:

```tactics
have h3 : ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure (compSL C₀((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) ℂ _ _ (cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y) (@ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap.ContinuousMap.liftZeroAtInftyCLE (spectrum ℂ a) ℂ ℂ _ _ _ _ _ _).symm.toContinuousLinearMap) f = 
  (cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y) f := by 
  apply ComplexRMK.integral_eq_apply
```

So `h3` is proven by `ComplexRMK.integral_eq_apply`.

Then we can rewrite:

```tactics
rw [h3]
```

Now we need to show that `(cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y) f = innerSL ℂ x ((cfcCLM ha f) y)`.

Looking at the definition of `cfc_toLinearFunctional`:

`cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f = innerSL ℂ x ((cfcCLM ha f) y)` by `cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply`.

So we can rewrite:

```tactics
simp [cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply]
```

Now we have `h1 : (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f = innerSL ℂ x ((cfcCLM ha f) y)`.

Next, we need to bound the norm of this.

We need to show `‖innerSL ℂ x ((cfcCLM ha f) y)‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖`.

Let's compute:

`‖innerSL ℂ x ((cfcCLM ha f) y)‖ = ‖innerSL ℂ x ((cfcCLM ha f) y)‖`

This is the norm of the inner product, which is the absolute value.

`|innerSL ℂ x ((cfcCLM ha f) y)| ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖(cfcCLM ha f) y‖` by `norm_inner_le_norm`.

Then, `‖(cfcCLM ha f) y‖ = ‖cfcCLM ha f‖ * ‖y‖` by `le_opNorm`.

And `‖cfcCLM ha f‖ = ‖f‖` because `cfcCLM ha` is an isometric isomorphism (it's a composition of `cfcHom` which is isometric and `apply'` which preserves norm).

Therefore, `‖(cfcCLM ha f) y‖ = ‖f‖ * ‖y‖`.

So `|innerSL ℂ x ((cfcCLM ha f) y)| ≤ ‖x‖ * (‖f‖ * ‖y‖)`.

Therefore, `‖innerSL ℂ x ((cfcCLM ha f) y)‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖`.

In Lean:

```tactics
rw [h1]
```

Now we need to bound `‖innerSL ℂ x ((cfcCLM ha f) y)‖`.

```tactics
have h2 : ‖innerSL ℂ x ((cfcCLM ha f) y)‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by
```

Let's prove this:

```tactics
have h3 : ‖innerSL ℂ x ((cfcCLM ha f) y)‖ = |innerSL ℂ x ((cfcCLM ha f) y)| := by
  simp [norm_eq_abs]
```

Next, we can apply the bound on the inner product:

```tactics
rw [h3]
have h4 : |innerSL ℂ x ((cfcCLM ha f) y)| ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖(cfcCLM ha f) y‖ := by
  apply abs_le.mp
  constructor
  · -- -‖x‖ * ‖(cfcCLM ha f) y‖ ≤ innerSL ℂ x ((cfcCLM ha f) y)
    linarith [neg_le_inner_le_norm x ((cfcCLM ha f) y), norm_nonneg ((cfcCLM ha f) y)]
  · -- innerSL ℂ x ((cfcCLM ha f) y) ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖(cfcCLM ha f) y‖
    linarith [inner_le_norm x ((cfcCLM ha f) y), norm_nonneg x]
```

Wait, this is too complicated. Let's use the existing lemma:

`norm_inner_le_norm` gives `‖innerSL ℂ x z‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖z‖`.

So we can apply this with `z = (cfcCLM ha f) y`.

```tactics
have h4 : |innerSL ℂ x ((cfcCLM ha f) y)| ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖(cfcCLM ha f) y‖ := by
  have h5 : ‖innerSL ℂ x ((cfcCLM ha f) y)‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖(cfcCLM ha f) y‖ := by
    apply norm_inner_le_norm
  have h6 : ‖innerSL ℂ x ((cfcCLM ha f) y)‖ = |innerSL ℂ x ((cfcCLM ha f) y)| := by
    simp [norm_eq_abs]
  linarith [h5, h6]
```

Next, we need to bound `‖(cfcCLM ha f) y‖`.

`‖(cfcCLM ha f) y‖ = ‖cfcCLM ha f‖ * ‖y‖` by `le_opNorm`.

And `‖cfcCLM ha f‖ = ‖f‖` by `norm_cfcCLM`.

So `‖(cfcCLM ha f) y‖ = ‖f‖ * ‖y‖`.

```tactics
have h5 : ‖(cfcCLM ha f) y‖ = ‖f‖ * ‖y‖ := by
  calc
    ‖(cfcCLM ha f) y‖ = ‖cfcCLM ha f‖ * ‖y‖ := by
      apply le_opNorm
      all_goals simp
    _ = ‖f‖ * ‖y‖ := by
      rw [norm_cfcCLM a f]
```

Now we can combine these:

```tactics
have h7 : ‖x‖ * ‖(cfcCLM ha f) y‖ = ‖x‖ * (‖f‖ * ‖y‖) := by
  rw [h5]
  all_goals ring
```

Finally, we can conclude:

```tactics
have h8 : ‖x‖ * (‖f‖ * ‖y‖) = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by
  ring
```

Now we can combine all the parts:

```tactics
calc
  |innerSL ℂ x ((cfcCLM ha f) y)| ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖(cfcCLM ha f) y‖ := h4
  _ = ‖x‖ * (‖f‖ * ‖y‖) := by rw [h5]
  _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by ring
```

So we have `h2 : ‖innerSL ℂ x ((cfcCLM ha f) y)‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖`.

Finally, we can conclude:

```tactics
linarith [h2]
```

Therefore, we've proven that `‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖`.

This completes the proof.

```lean
import Mathlib
import SpectralThm.RieszMarkovKakutani.Complex
import SpectralThm.Resolutions
-- import SpectralThm.WStarAlgebra.BorelFunctionalCalculus

/-!
# The Spectral Theorem

-/

@[expose] public section

open InnerProductSpace ContinuousLinearMap ZeroAtInfty ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap MeasureTheory

noncomputable section

variable {H : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup H] [InnerProductSpace ℂ H] [CompleteSpace H]
  {a : H →L[ℂ] H} (ha : IsSelfAdjoint a)

variable (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ))

/-- For `x y : H`, consider `b => ⟪x, b y⟫_ℂ` as a bounded linear functional in `b`. -/
def toLinearFunctional (x y : H) : (H →L[ℂ] H) →L[ℂ] ℂ
    := compSL (H →L[ℂ] H) _ _ _ _ (innerSL ℂ x) (apply' H _ y)

@[simp]
lemma toLinearFunctional_apply (x y : H) {b : H →L[ℂ] H}:
    toLinearFunctional x y b = ⟪x, (b y)⟫_ℂ := rfl

/-- The linear functional `b => ⟪x, b y⟫_ℂ` is bounded by `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`. -/
lemma toLinearFunctional_bounded (x y : H) : ‖toLinearFunctional x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
  grw [ContinuousLinearMap.opNorm_le_bound]
  . positivity
  . intro a
    simp only [toLinearFunctional_apply]
    grw [norm_inner_le_norm, ContinuousLinearMap.le_opNorm]
    apply le_of_eq
    ring

/-- Let `a` a normal bounded operator. By the continuous functional calculus, there is an injective
homomorphism from `C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)` to  `H →L[ℂ] H`, informally denoted by `f => f(a)`. This
definition just makes it a continuous linear map.
cf. Rudin "Functional Analysis" Theorem 11.18, the proof of Theorem 12.23, first paragraph.
-/
def cfcCLM : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →L[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] H
    := ((cfcHom ha.isStarNormal : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →⋆ₐ H →L[ℂ] H).toLinearMap).mkContinuous 1
        ( by simp only [AlgHom.toLinearMap_apply, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom, one_mul];
              exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))

/-- The same map `f => f(a)` as a continuous algebra homomorphism.
-/
def cfcCAH : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →A[ℂ] H →L[ℂ] H
    := ContinuousAlgHom.mk (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →⋆ₐ H →L[ℂ] H).toAlgHom
        (by apply @continuous_of_linear_of_bound _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal).toAlgHom.map_add'
              (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal).toLinearMap.map_smul' 1
            simp only [AlgHom.toRingHom_eq_coe, RingHom.toMonoidHom_eq_coe,
              AlgHom.toRingHom_toMonoidHom, OneHom.toFun_eq_coe, MonoidHom.toOneHom_coe,
              MonoidHom.coe_coe, StarAlgHom.coe_toAlgHom, one_mul]
            exact fun f => le_of_eq (norm_cfcHom a f))

@[simp]
lemma cfcCLM_apply (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) : cfcCLM ha f = cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f := rfl

/-- The composition of the linear functional and the continuous functional,
`f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ` as a continuous linear functional. -/
def cfc_toLinearFunctional (x y : H) : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) →L[ℂ] ℂ
    := compSL C(spectrum ℂ a, ℂ) _ _ _ _ (toLinearFunctional x y) (cfcCLM ha)

@[simp]
lemma cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply (f : C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) (x y : H) :
    cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f = ⟪x, (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f y)⟫_ℂ := rfl

/-- For each pair `x y : H`, the linear map `f => ⟪x, f(a) y⟫_ℂ`has norm `‖x‖ * ‖y‖`.
This follows from the bound

have h1 : ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y‖.totalVariation * ‖f‖ := by
    apply norm_le_norm_of_le_norm
    apply ComplexMeasure.norm_integrals_le_total_variation
  have h2 : ‖cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y‖.totalVariation = ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ := by
    simp [cfc_toComplexMeasure, ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure, ContinuousLinearMap.opNorm_eq_total_variation]
    <;> try { norm_num }
  have h3 : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound x y
  rw [h2] at h1
  nlinarith

have h1 : (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f = cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f := by
    simp [cfc_toComplexMeasure, ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure_apply]
  rw [h1]
  have h2 : cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f = ⟪x, (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f y)⟫_ℂ := by
    simp [cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply]
  rw [h2]
  have h3 : ‖⟪x, (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f y)⟫_ℂ‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by
    have h4 : ‖⟪x, (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f y)⟫_ℂ‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖(cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f y)‖ := by
      apply norm_inner_le_norm
    have h5 : ‖(cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f y)‖ = ‖cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f‖ * ‖y‖ := by
      simp [norm_mul]
    have h6 : ‖cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f‖ = ‖f‖ := by
      simp [norm_cfcHom]
    calc
      ‖⟪x, (cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f y)⟫_ℂ‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖(cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f y)‖ := by
        apply norm_inner_le_norm
      _ = ‖x‖ * (‖cfcHom ha.isStarNormal f‖ * ‖y‖) := by
        rw [h5]
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖f‖ * ‖y‖ := by
        rw [h6]
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by
        ring
  exact h3

have h1 : ComplexMeasure.integral (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y) f =
      cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f := by
    rw [show cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y = ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure (compSL C₀((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)
      C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ) ℂ _ _ (cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y)
      (@ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap.ContinuousMap.liftZeroAtInftyCLE
      (spectrum ℂ a) ℂ ℂ _ _ _ _ _ _).symm.toContinuousLinearMap) by rfl]
    simp [ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure_apply]
  rw [h1]
  have h2 : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by
    have h3 : cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f = ⟪x, (cfcCLM ha f) y⟫_ℂ := by
      simp [cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply, cfcCLM_apply]
    rw [h3]
    have h4 : ‖⟪x, (cfcCLM ha f) y⟫_ℂ‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖(cfcCLM ha f) y‖ := by
      apply norm_inner_le_norm
    have h5 : ‖(cfcCLM ha f) y‖ ≤ ‖cfcCLM ha f‖ * ‖y‖ := by
      apply norm_le_norm_apply
    have h6 : ‖cfcCLM ha f‖ = ‖f‖ := by
      simp [cfcCLM_apply, norm_cfcHom]
    have h7 : ‖(cfcCLM ha f) y‖ ≤ ‖f‖ * ‖y‖ := by
      rw [h6] at h5
      linarith
    calc
      ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f‖ = ‖⟪x, (cfcCLM ha f) y⟫_ℂ‖ := by rw [h3]
      _ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖(cfcCLM ha f) y‖ := by apply h4
      _ ≤ ‖x‖ * (‖f‖ * ‖y‖) := by rw [h7]
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by ring
  exact h2

have h1 : (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f = cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f := by
    simp [cfc_toComplexMeasure]
    <;> try { 
      simp_all [ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure_apply]
      <;> try { 
        norm_num [ContinuousLinearMap.comp_apply]
      }
    }
    <;> try { 
      norm_num [ContinuousLinearMap.comp_apply]
    }
  rw [h1]
  have h2 : cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f = ⟪x, (cfcCLM ha f) y⟫_ℂ := by 
    simp [cfc_toLinearFunctional]
  rw [h2]
  have h3 : ‖⟪x, (cfcCLM ha f) y⟫_ℂ‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖(cfcCLM ha f) y‖ := by 
    apply norm_inner_le_norm
  have h4 : ‖(cfcCLM ha f) y‖ ≤ ‖f‖ * ‖y‖ := by 
    calc 
      ‖(cfcCLM ha f) y‖ = ‖cfcCLM ha f‖ * ‖y‖ := by 
        rw [norm_mul]
      _ = ‖f‖ * ‖y‖ := by 
        have h5 : ‖cfcCLM ha f‖ = ‖f‖ := by 
          simp [cfcCLM]
          <;> try { 
            simp_all [norm_cfcHom]
          }
          <;> try { 
            norm_num [norm_cfcHom]
          }
        rw [h5]
  calc 
    ‖⟪x, (cfcCLM ha f) y⟫_ℂ‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖(cfcCLM ha f) y‖ := h3
    _ ≤ ‖x‖ * (‖f‖ * ‖y‖) := by 
      apply mul_le_mul_of_nonneg_left h4 
      all_goals 
        positivity 
    _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by 
      ring

calc
    ‖ComplexMeasure.integral (@cfc_toComplexMeasure H inst✝² inst✝¹ inst✝ a ha x y) ⇑f‖ = ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f‖ := by
      simp [ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure_apply, cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply]
    _ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by
      apply le_trans (cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound x y)
      all_goals norm_num

have h1 : ComplexMeasure.integral (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y) f = cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f := by 
    simp [cfc_toComplexMeasure, ComplexRMK.integral_of_compSL]
  rw [h1]
  have h2 : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y f‖ ≤ ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ * ‖f‖ := by
    apply norm_le_mul
  have h3 : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ * ‖f‖ ≤ (‖x‖ * ‖y‖) * ‖f‖ := by
    have h4 : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound x y
    nlinarith [norm_nonneg (cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y), norm_nonneg f, h4]
  linarith [h2, h3]

have h3 : ‖(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by
    have h4 : (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f = (cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y) f := by
      simp [cfc_toComplexMeasure, ComplexRMK.rieszMeasure_apply]
      all_goals try { tauto }
      all_goals try { aesop }
    rw [h4]
    have h5 : ‖(cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y) f‖ ≤ ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ * ‖f‖ := by
      apply norm_le_mul_norm
    have h6 : ‖cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ := by
      apply cfc_toLinearFunctional_bound
    have h7 : ‖(cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y) f‖ ≤ (‖x‖ * ‖y‖) * ‖f‖ := by
      linarith [h5, h6]
    calc
      ‖(cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y) f‖ = ‖(cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y) f‖ := by rfl
      _ ≤ (‖x‖ * ‖y‖) * ‖f‖ := h7
      _ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * ‖f‖ := by ring
  exact h3

exact h3
